title: Digital Detox Gains Momentum
subtitle: 为什么越来越多人选择断网生活

paragraphs.en: Digital detox—deliberately disconnecting from electronic devices—has emerged as a growing movement among people overwhelmed by constant connectivity. Retreats offering technology-free environments attract participants seeking relief from notification anxiety and screen addiction. This trend reflects widespread concern about technology's impact on mental health and human relationships.
paragraphs.zh: 数字排毒——有意识地与电子设备断开连接——已成为被持续连接压垮的人们中一个不断增长的运动。提供无技术环境的静修活动吸引着寻求从通知焦虑和屏幕成瘾中解脱的参与者。这一趋势反映了对技术对心理健康和人际关系影响的广泛担忧。

paragraphs.en: Research supports the benefits of periodic disconnection. Studies show that breaks from social media reduce anxiety and depression symptoms while improving sleep quality. Face-to-face conversations become more engaging when phones are absent, and attention spans recover from the fragmentation caused by constant interruptions.
paragraphs.zh: 研究支持定期断网的好处。研究表明，从社交媒体休息可以减少焦虑和抑郁症状，同时改善睡眠质量。当手机不在时，面对面的对话变得更加投入，注意力也从持续中断造成的碎片化中恢复。

paragraphs.en: Many people report rediscovering forgotten pleasures during digital detoxes. Reading physical books, engaging in creative hobbies, and simply being present with thoughts become more accessible without digital distractions. Some describe these experiences as reclaiming time and attention that had been unconsciously surrendered.
paragraphs.zh: 许多人报告说在数字排毒期间重新发现了被遗忘的乐趣。阅读实体书、从事创意爱好，以及简单地与自己的思想共处，在没有数字干扰的情况下变得更加容易。一些人将这些经历描述为收回了不知不觉中放弃的时间和注意力。

paragraphs.en: Complete disconnection proves impractical for most people given work requirements and family communication needs. Many advocates therefore recommend more sustainable approaches: designated phone-free times, removing social media apps while keeping essential functions, or establishing tech-free zones within homes.
paragraphs.zh: 考虑到工作要求和家庭沟通需求，对大多数人来说，完全断网被证明是不切实际的。因此，许多倡导者推荐更可持续的方法：指定无手机时间、删除社交媒体应用同时保留基本功能，或在家中建立无技术区域。

paragraphs.en: The digital detox movement ultimately advocates for intentional technology use rather than complete rejection. Technology offers tremendous benefits when employed consciously, but passive consumption often leads to dissatisfaction and wasted time. Developing healthier digital habits requires recognizing when devices serve us versus when we serve them.
paragraphs.zh: 数字排毒运动最终倡导的是有意识地使用技术，而非完全拒绝。当有意识地使用时，技术提供巨大的好处，但被动消费往往导致不满和浪费时间。培养更健康的数字习惯需要认识到设备何时在服务我们，何时我们在服务它们。
